title: Wire `relayhq init` subcommand into the CLI
implementation_notes: Add 'init' to executeRelayHQInvocation() in cli/relayhq.ts, before the 'help' fallback.
If --vault-root is missing, print a clear usage error and exit 1.
On alreadyExists: print 'vault/shared/ already exists at <path>. Use an empty directory.' and exit 1.
On success: print the list of created files, then print the suggested .env line: RELAYHQ_VAULT_ROOT=<path>
Add the 'init' command to renderRelayHQHelp() and RELAYHQ_COMMAND_SURFACE.
The command must NOT require a running RelayHQ server — it works offline, writing files directly.
acceptance_criteria: `bun run ./cli/relayhq.ts init --vault-root=/tmp/test-vault` creates vault/shared/ and prints created files.
`bun run ./cli/relayhq.ts init` (no flag) exits 1 with a usage message.
Running init twice on the same path exits 1 with 'already exists' message.
Output includes the exact RELAYHQ_VAULT_ROOT=... line the user should add to .env.
No HTTP call is made during init.
completion_summary: Wired relayhq init into the CLI with offline scaffold flow, help entry, and clear init errors.
